titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L735
GA L735
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Available for noncommercial re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators and the subsequent derivative is licensed as ShareAlike (Creative Commons by-nc-sa)

projectDesc.p: This transcription was initially made by the Byzantine Text of John Project in 2004-7, led by Roderic L. Mullen, and later adopted for the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John.

The IGNTP transcription file was duplicated for the standalone version of the Byzantine Text of John on 9.7.2014. This means that there may be slight discrepancies between the 2007 file used to prepared the Byzantine apparatus and the 2014 transcription in the present file.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: 19.24 and 25 changed to 21.24 and 25 and 17.14-1 split into 17.18 and 17.14 by Rachel Kevern.
πατερ moved from 17.16-1 to 17.17 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 17.11 (x2) changed and 17.18 split into 17.14, 15, 16, 17 and 18 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 11.54 changed by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-A number: L735 (Athos, Ρrotatou 20) (Νinth Century)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1r Τranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 25.06.02Τranscription finished: 09.11.02 Τransference to Collate by: RLMΤransference to Collate begun: 25.06.02Τransference to Collate finished: 09.11.02File date and time:Ρlaced in Level1: RLMLevel1 file date and time: 09.11.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Karavidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Menologion read in MuensterΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 02.12.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time:Page layout added by: acmStart date: 15.9.13 End date: 19.9.13Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: ---αγ. και μεγ. Ε., εσπερασ, ευαγ. Α - ΙΒ, - follows Ηoly Saturday rdgs - see F 140r---εωθινον rdgs follow the αγ. και μεγ. Ε., εσπερασ, ευαγ. ΙΒ rdg---Ηoly Week ορθρος rdgs follow the εωθινον rdgs, see F 169r---Μενολ. starts 182r---many odd itacismsUnidentified reading 1 is on F188r: M1 D7c or M1 D14? John 3, 13-17.Unidentified reading 2 is on F223r: M9 D11? John 15, 9-16.Unidentified reading 3 is on F229r: M11 D16? John 17,11-21.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
This lection has no heading and follows straight on from Matthew. A later hand adds to the margin: τη νηπτηρος
This lection has no heading and follows straight on from Matthew.
The manuscript mistakenly ascribes this next lection to Matthew.
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)

ab.note: The text contains a reference to locate the reading for R9: John 20,19-31.
The text contains a reference to locate the reading for R11: John 21,14-21.

div.note: The heading instructs the user to read this lection on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
The manuscript mistakenly ascribes this next lection to Luke.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
